config.title: Admin Settings
config.subtitle: Workspace-wide settings for DQX Studio.
config.adminOnly: Only admins can change this setting
config.labelKeyInvalid: Key "{{key}}" must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, and underscores.
config.keyHint: Letters, digits, underscore. Must start with a letter.
config.weightHint: Drives the weight picker on rule authoring pages.
config.builtinKeyLockedTooltip: Built-in key — the name and description can't be changed.
config.severityDescriptionPlaceholder: Default rule severity of a rule, overridable on a per-table basis.
config.failedSaveDraftSample: Failed to save the draft run sample limit.
config.aiSettingsEnabledHint: Enables AI features across the app.
config.aiSettingsEndpointHint: Model serving endpoint used for AI features.
config.aiSettingsAdminOnlyHint: Only admins can change this setting.
config.rulesRegistrySettingsDescription: Control how new rule attachments start out, and what happens when a followed rule changes.
config.defaultAutoUpgradeHint: When applying a new rule to a table, default to always running the "latest" version of that rule. Can be overridden on a per-rule basis.
config.defaultAutoUpgradeHintOff: When applying a new rule to a table, default to always explicitly pinning the most recent version of that rule. Can be overridden on a per-rule basis.
config.autoUpgradeWithoutApprovalLabel: Bypass approvals for automatic rule upgrades
config.autoUpgradeWithoutApprovalHint: Automatically approve changes to tables when rule versions are upgraded
config.tagAutoApplyHint: When on, automatically apply rules matching Unity Catalog tags of the column in question. When off, offer these rules as suggestions instead
config.passThresholdSettingsDescription: Warn when fewer than a set % of tests pass a check. Set the workspace default here; override it per rule and per column when applying rules.
config.rulesRegistryPassThresholdEnabledHint: When on, warn if fewer than a set % of tests pass for a given rule/column
config.rulesRegistryDefaultPassThresholdHint: Checks warn when fewer than this % of tests pass. Overridable per rule and per column.
config.rulesRegistrySettingsFailedSave: Failed to save Rules Registry settings.
config.rulesRegistrySettingsAdminOnlyHint: Only admins can change these settings.
config.approvalsModeDescription: Control the review gate for submitting rules, registry rules, monitored tables, and table spaces.
config.approvalsMode_enabled_hint: Every submit requires review before publishing.
config.approvalsMode_auto_bypass_hint: A submit publishes immediately when the submitter could approve it themselves; everyone else still waits for review.
config.approvalsMode_disabled_hint: Every submit publishes immediately with no review step.
config.approvalsModeAdminOnlyHint: Only admins can change this setting.
config.computeSpAccessMissing: The app service principal can't use this warehouse yet.
config.computeJobsClusterNote: Saved for future use. Check jobs currently run on serverless.
config.computeAdminOnlyHint: Only admins can change compute settings.
config.permissionsDefaultInheritHint: When granting permission to a table space or monitored table, also by default grant SELECT permissions to underlying monitored tables and rules
config.permissionsDefaultInheritAdminOnlyHint: Only admins can change this setting.
config.globalResultsHint: Adds a results link to the sidebar
config.globalResultsFailedSave: Failed to save Global Results setting.
config.globalResultsAdminOnlyHint: Only admins can change this setting.
config.requireDraftRunLabel: Require a draft run before submit
config.requireDraftRunHint: Require a test/draft run before a rule, monitored table, or table space can be submitted for review, so checks are tested first
config.requireDraftRunFailedSave: Failed to save the draft-run requirement.
config.requireDraftRunAdminOnlyHint: Only admins can change this setting.
config.resetDbWarningHeading: This permanently deletes all DQX Studio data
config.resetDbAdminOnly: Only admins can reset the database.
config.resetDbDialogBody: This will permanently delete all DQX Studio data. There is no undo. To confirm, type the phrase below exactly.
config.resetDbPartial: Database reset, but {{count}} table(s) could not be cleared. See server logs.
config.demoBody: Populate this workspace with a realistic sample data-quality deployment — sample tables, rules across every quality dimension, and several weeks of run history.
config.demoAdminOnly: Only admins can deploy demo content.
config.demoWarning: This takes about 30 minutes to populate. Don't interact with the app while it runs.
config.demoStarted: Demo deployment started. It will take about 30 minutes.
config.demoFailed: Demo deployment failed. See server logs.
config.kwTimezone: timezone time zone display clock date
config.kwReviewStatuses: run review status label acknowledge resolve triage
config.kwAi: ai artificial intelligence llm suggester generate endpoint gateway
config.kwLabels: labels rule tags dimension severity weight metadata
config.kwRulesRegistry: rules registry governance version upgrade approval pin follow
config.kwPassThreshold: pass threshold quality gate warn tests percent breach
config.kwApprovalsMode: approvals mode approve review gate submit publish auto bypass workflow
config.kwRequireDraftRun: require draft run before submit dry run validate test gate approval
config.kwEntitlements: entitlements roles groups rbac access permissions mapping admin approver author viewer runner
config.kwPermissions: permissions grants inheritance object access sharing
config.kwGlobalResults: global results tab sidebar nav overall score visibility enable show hide observability
config.kwCompute: compute warehouse cluster serverless jobs sql
config.kwDraftSample: draft run sample limit rows sampling
config.kwRetention: retention data delete cleanup quarantine days history
config.kwDanger: danger zone reset database wipe clear delete destroy destructive factory reset erase
config.computeWarehouseHint: Used for data previews and ad-hoc queries.
config.computeJobsHint: Compute used for profile and check jobs. Serverless is the lowest-touch option.
config.draftSampleAdminOnlyHint: Only admins can change this setting.
config.governedKeyError: Could not load governed tag keys.

discovery.title: Discovery
discovery.subtitle: Browse Unity Catalog assets to find tables for your data quality checks.
discovery.browseCatalogDescription: Select a catalog, schema, and table to view its columns.
discovery.selectTableHint: Choose a catalog, schema, and table above to view its column definitions.
discovery.failedLoadRules: Failed to load rules for this table.
discovery.noRulesYet: No quality rules defined for this table yet.
discovery.getStartedAi: Get started by generating rules with AI.
discovery.noColumns: No columns found for this table.

profiler.title: Profile & generate
profiler.subtitle: Profile tables to generate data quality rule suggestions based on data distribution.
profiler.newRunDescription: Select one or more tables (or an entire schema), configure sampling, and run the profiler.
profiler.rowFilterHint: Optional SQL condition applied before profiling.
profiler.toggleColumnsHint: Click to toggle. No selection = profile all columns.
profiler.removeOutliersHint: Exclude statistical outliers when computing min/max range checks.
profiler.outlierThresholdHint: Standard deviations from mean to consider an outlier (default: 3).
profiler.llmPkHint: Use AI to detect primary key columns and generate uniqueness checks.
profiler.noRuns: No profiling runs yet. Run the profiler above to get started.
profiler.submittedWaiting: Profiling job submitted — waiting for results...
profiler.permissionHint: Ask your workspace admin to grant the required Unity Catalog permissions, then try again.
profiler.permissionHintBatch: Ask your workspace admin to grant the required Unity Catalog permissions on the selected tables, then try again.
profiler.permissionHintTable: Ask your workspace admin to grant the required Unity Catalog permissions on this table.
profiler.moreFailed: … and {{count}} more table(s) failed to submit
profiler.noRulesGenerated: No rules were generated from the profiling data.

columnStats.title: Column statistics ({{count}})
columnStats.footnote: Stats are computed by the DQX profiler on the sampled subset of rows — use the Run history → View dialog to compare against past runs.

rulesActive.title: Active rules
rulesActive.subtitle: Approved rules currently enforced on your tables.
rulesActive.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this rule for
rulesActive.noColumnRules: No column / dataset rules found. Switch to "Cross-table" to view query-based rules.
rulesActive.noCrossTableHint: Cross-table SQL rules will appear here once created and approved.
rulesActive.noActiveRulesAuthor: Create and approve rules to see them here. Active rules are enforced during quality runs.
rulesActive.noActiveRulesViewer: No rules have been approved yet. Check Drafts & review for pending rule sets.

rulesCreate.title: Create Rules
rulesCreate.subtitle: Choose how you want to create data quality rules.
rulesCreate.singleTableDescription: Define column-level, schema-validation, and reference-table checks (e.g. foreign_key) for individual tables, with optional AI assistance.
rulesCreate.crossTableDescription: Write SQL queries that validate data across multiple tables or run dataset-level aggregation checks.
rulesCreate.profileDescription: Profile your data to auto-generate quality rules based on column statistics.
rulesCreate.importDescription: Bulk-import from a DQX YAML file or generate rules from an ODCS v3.x data contract.
rulesCreate.fromContractDescription: Generate rules from an ODCS v3.x data contract — schema, constraints, and explicit DQX checks.
rulesCreate.schemaDescription: Catch drift in columns, order, and types with a has_valid_schema rule — strict match or compatibility check.

rulesFromContract.title: Generate rules from data contract
rulesFromContract.subtitle: Upload an Open Data Contract Standard (ODCS) v3.x contract and DQX will derive quality rules from its schema, constraints, and explicit checks.

input.title: Contract source
input.description: Provide an ODCS v3.x contract in YAML or JSON. Schema validation requires every property to have a physicalType set to a valid Unity Catalog type.

options.predefinedHint: Derive is_not_null, is_unique, regex_match, range and length checks from property constraints.
options.schemaValidationHint: Emit a has_valid_schema rule per ODCS schema using each property's physicalType.
options.strictHint: Require exact column set, order, and types. Disable for permissive validation that allows extra columns.
options.textRulesHint: Convert the contract's text expectations (type: text) into checks using AI. Slower — each expectation is sent to the language model.
options.criticalityHint: Applied to predefined rules. Explicit rules keep the criticality declared in the contract.
options.severityHint: Applied to predefined rules. Explicit rules keep the severity declared in the contract.

rulesFromContract.generateHint: DQX parses the contract locally — no data is read from your workspace. Enable natural-language expectations to also convert the contract's free-text quality rules with AI.

errors.emptyContract: Paste or upload a contract first.
errors.generateFailed: Failed to generate rules from contract
errors.noRules: No rules were generated to import.

results.description: Review the generated rules, then save them to the Rules Registry as drafts.
results.noSchemas: The contract didn't define any schemas, so nothing was generated.
results.saveSummaryRegistry_one: {{count}} rule ready to import to the registry
results.saveSummaryRegistry_other: {{count}} rules ready to import to the registry
results.noRulesToSave: No rules were generated to save.
results.unassignedHint: These rules can't be auto-bucketed; they will be skipped unless the contract is updated.

schema.targetTableHint: Required. Rules will be saved as drafts against this table.
schema.noRules: No rules generated for this schema.
schema.missingTitle_one: {{count}} contract field is missing on the target table
schema.missingTitle_other: {{count}} contract fields are missing on the target table
schema.missingBody: These fields are referenced by the generated rules but do not exist on {{table}}:
schema.missingHint: Pick a different target table, or update the contract so its field names match the table.
schema.inspectErrorTitle: Could not list columns for the chosen table
schema.inspectErrorBody: Verify the table exists in Unity Catalog and that you have permission to read its metadata.

rulesBulkImport.title: Bulk contract import
rulesBulkImport.subtitle: Upload multiple ODCS contracts to register their tables, create rules in the registry, and auto-apply them in one step.

entryCard.title: Bulk import from data contracts
entryCard.description: Upload several ODCS contracts at once — register tables, create rules, and auto-apply where possible.

upload.title: Contracts
upload.description: Add one or more ODCS v3.x contract files (YAML or JSON). Each schema becomes a target table.
upload.dropzone: Drag & drop contract files here, or

target.title: Target location
target.description: Schemas without a fully-qualified physical name in their contract are registered under this catalog and schema.

rulesBulkImport.confirmDescription: This registers {{count}} tables, creates their rules in the registry, and applies the approved ones. It runs synchronously and may take a while for large bundles.

preview.title: Import plan
preview.description: Review the resolved tables and auto-map coverage before running the import.
preview.unresolvedWarning: Some schemas couldn't be resolved to a table. Their rules will be created in the registry but not applied.
preview.applyNote: Only approved rules are auto-applied. Rules awaiting approval, or with columns that can't be auto-matched, are surfaced in the results for manual follow-up.

errors.noFiles: Add at least one contract file.
errors.noTarget: Choose a target catalog and schema.
errors.batchFailed: A batch of rules failed to import.
errors.applyFailed: Failed to apply a rule to {{fqn}}

results.title: Import results
results.description: Per-contract summary. Expand a row to see errors and tables that need manual follow-up.
results.attentionHint: Awaiting-approval rules auto-apply once the rule is approved (shown on the table's Apply Rules tab). Need-mapping rules must be added and mapped there manually.

rulesImport.title: Import rules
rulesImport.subtitle: Import data quality rules from a YAML file.
rulesImport.subtitleYaml: Bulk-import rules from a DQX YAML file.
rulesImport.subtitleContract: Generate rules from an ODCS v3.x data contract — schema, constraints, and explicit DQX checks.
rulesImport.importDescription: Upload a YAML file or paste YAML content. Each entry should follow the DQX check format. Rules are saved to the Rules Registry — assign them to tables later from Tables.
rulesImport.registryHint: Rules are saved to the Rules Registry as drafts. Assign them to monitored tables after approval.
rulesImport.yamlMustBeList:
YAML must be a list of check definitions, for example:
- criticality: error
  check:
    function: is_not_null
    arguments:
      column: id
rulesImport.yamlCommentsOnly: Only comments were found — paste at least one rule starting with a dash (-).
rulesImport.yamlEmptyList: The YAML list is empty — add at least one rule entry.
rulesImport.yamlInvalidEntry: Rule {{index}} is incomplete or invalid. Each list item must be a check object with a check.function field.
rulesImport.selectTargetTable: Select a target table to dry-run against
rulesImport.noBoundChecks: No table-bound checks to dry-run (cross-table SQL checks are skipped)
rulesImport.dryRunSubmitted: Dry run submitted — waiting for results...
rulesImport.failedFetchDryRun: Failed to fetch dry run results
rulesImport.failedCheckDryRunStatus: Failed to check dry run status
rulesImport.reusedExisting_one: Reused {{count}} existing rule (identical rule already in the registry)
rulesImport.reusedExisting_other: Reused {{count}} existing rules (identical rules already in the registry)
rulesImport.submitFailed_one: {{count}} rule failed to submit — saved as drafts instead
rulesImport.submitFailed_other: {{count}} rules failed to submit — saved as drafts instead
rulesImport.dryRunTooltip: Dry-run validates only the first 1–10,000 rows of the target table — it's a fast preview, not a full validation. To run imported rules against every row after approval, go to Run Rules and pick "All rows".

rulesCreateSql.title: Cross-table rules
rulesCreateSql.subtitle: Write SQL queries that validate data across tables or run dataset-level aggregation checks.
rulesCreateSql.generateWithAiHint: Describe the cross-table validation you need. Use fully qualified table names (catalog.schema.table) so the AI can write accurate SQL.
rulesCreateSql.generateWithAiPlaceholder: e.g. Check that every order in catalog.schema.orders has at least one line item in catalog.schema.line_items, and that the order total matches the sum of line item amounts
rulesCreateSql.duplicateTooltip: A SQL check with this name already exists. Remove or rename it.
rulesCreateSql.queryDescription: The query should return rows that violate the check (i.e., bad rows).
rulesCreateSql.nameLengthError: Name must be 128 characters or fewer.
rulesCreateSql.nameInvalidError: Name must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, and underscores.
rulesCreateSql.nameHint: Letters, numbers, and underscores only. Must start with a letter.
rulesCreateSql.querySemicolonError: Semicolons are not allowed. Enter a single SELECT query only.
rulesCreateSql.queryProhibitedError: Query contains prohibited keywords (DROP, DELETE, INSERT, etc.). Only SELECT queries are allowed.
rulesCreateSql.sqlQueryPlaceholder:
SELECT o.order_id, o.total, SUM(li.amount) AS line_total
FROM catalog.schema.orders o
JOIN catalog.schema.line_items li ON li.order_id = o.order_id
GROUP BY o.order_id, o.total
HAVING o.total != line_total
rulesCreateSql.sqlQueryHint: Use fully qualified table names (catalog.schema.table). Returned rows are treated as violations.
rulesCreateSql.everyCheckNeedsNameQuery: Every check needs a name and a SQL query
rulesCreateSql.checksAlreadyExist_one: {{count}} check already exists and cannot be saved
rulesCreateSql.checksAlreadyExist_other: {{count}} checks already exist and cannot be saved
rulesCreateSql.dryRunInProgressDescription: A dry run is currently running. Leaving will cancel it and discard any results.
rulesCreateSql.unsavedSqlChecks: You have unsaved SQL checks. Leaving will discard your changes.
rulesCreateSql.assignToTableOn: This rule will be grouped with the selected table's rules, making it available for that table's pipeline.
rulesCreateSql.assignToTableOff: Saved as a standalone cross-table check by default.
rulesCreateSql.enterSqlBeforeDryRun: Enter a SQL query before running a dry run
rulesCreateSql.dryRunSubmitted: Dry run submitted — waiting for results...
rulesCreateSql.notSqlRule: This rule isn't a cross-table SQL check, so it can't be edited here.
rulesCreateSql.noSqlGenerated: AI did not generate any SQL checks. Try being more specific about the tables and conditions.
rulesCreateSql.fetchResultsFailed: Dry run succeeded but failed to fetch results. Try refreshing.
rulesCreateSql.unknownDryRunError: Dry run failed with an unknown error.

diff.title: Review changes
diff.rrNoPrior: This rule has no published version yet — showing the proposed definition being reviewed.
diff.mtFirstApproval: This table has never been approved — there is no frozen version to compare against yet. It will be frozen as v1 on approval.
diff.mtNoPrior: This is the table's first frozen version — there is no earlier version to compare against.
diff.tsNoSnapshot: Spaces don't keep per-version snapshots, so there is no prior version to diff. Showing the current proposed members and their governed checks.
diff.ruleNoPrior: No earlier recorded change for this rule — showing its current definition.

rulesSingleTable.title: Single table rules
rulesSingleTable.subtitle: Define column-level checks for an individual table.
rulesSingleTable.editSubtitle: Editing rules for {{table}}. Run a dry run or save changes.
rulesSingleTable.createSubtitle: Define rules with AI or manually, assign tables, then validate and save.
rulesSingleTable.validateSaveDescription: Run a dry run against a specific table to validate, then save rules.
rulesSingleTable.referenceTableLabel: Reference table (optional — provides column context to AI)
rulesSingleTable.aiPromptPlaceholder: e.g. All ID columns must not be null, email must be valid format, amounts must be positive
rulesSingleTable.sampleRowsTooltip: Dry-run validates only the first 1–10,000 rows of the selected table — it's a fast preview, not a full validation. To run rules against every row, go to Run Rules and pick "All rows".
rulesSingleTable.selectFunctionForEvery: Select a function for every check
rulesSingleTable.assignAtLeastOneTable: Assign at least one target table to a check
rulesSingleTable.dryRunInProgressDescription: A dry run is currently running. Leaving will cancel it and discard any results.
rulesSingleTable.unsavedChangesDescription: You have unsaved rule changes. Leaving will discard your edits.
rulesSingleTable.duplicateTooltip: This check already exists for the selected table(s). Remove or modify it.
rulesSingleTable.mutexUsingOther: Using "{{other}}" — clear it to use this field instead.
rulesSingleTable.mutexConflict: Provide either this or "{{other}}", not both.
rulesSingleTable.toastAllChecksHaveTarget: All checks already have a target table.
rulesSingleTable.toastAllChecksHaveTargetDescription: Use a check's "Target tables" panel to add {{table}} explicitly.
rulesSingleTable.toastTargetedChecksDescription: Checks with explicit targets were left unchanged.
rulesSingleTable.toastAiNoChecks: AI did not generate any valid checks. Try a different description.
rulesSingleTable.toastAiFailed: AI generation failed. Please try again.
rulesSingleTable.toastSelectTable: Select a table to run a dry run against
rulesSingleTable.toastNoChecksTargetTable: No checks target the selected table
rulesSingleTable.toastDryRunSubmitted: Dry run submitted — waiting for results...
rulesSingleTable.toastDryRunFetchFailed: Failed to fetch dry run results
rulesSingleTable.toastFailedCheckStatus: Failed to check dry run status
rulesSingleTable.toastOriginalTargetRemoved: {{table}}: original target was removed in the editor but the rule was kept. Delete it from Drafts if you no longer want it.
rulesSingleTable.toastDuplicatesExist:
Duplicate rules already exist and cannot be saved:
{{messages}}
rulesSingleTable.argInvalidColumnName: "{{name}}" is not a valid column name. Use letters, numbers, underscores, and dots only.
rulesSingleTable.argSemicolonsListNotAllowed: Semicolons are not allowed in list values.
rulesSingleTable.argSemicolonsExpressionNotAllowed: Semicolons are not allowed in expressions.
rulesSingleTable.argExpressionProhibitedKeyword: Expression contains a prohibited SQL keyword (DROP, DELETE, INSERT, etc.).
rulesSingleTable.crossArgRangeNeedsBound: Provide at least one of Min Limit or Max Limit.
rulesSingleTable.argHintIsUniqueColumns: comma-separated, e.g. id or col1, col2
rulesSingleTable.overrideSchemaColumnsHelp: Optional. Restrict the schema check to these columns; leave empty to validate the whole table against the expected schema.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpNullsDistinct: When true (default), NULL values are treated as distinct from each other.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpTrimStrings: When true, treat whitespace-only values as empty.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpCaseSensitive: When false, the comparison is case-insensitive.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpDateFormat: Optional. Leave blank to use DQX's default ISO date parser.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpTimestampFormat: Optional. Leave blank to use DQX's default ISO timestamp parser.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpNegateExpression: When true, the rule fails when the expression evaluates to true.
rulesSingleTable.overrideHelpNegateRegex: When true, the rule fails when the value matches the pattern.

runsHistory.title: Runs History
runsHistory.subtitle: View past validation and profiling runs.
runsHistory.runByAria: Filter by who ran the run
runsHistory.permissionsDescription: You don't have permission to view run history. Please contact your administrator.
runsHistory.adjustFiltersHint: Try adjusting your filters to find runs.
runsHistory.executeRulesHint: Execute approved rules from the Run Rules page to see results here.
runsHistory.skeletonFailedHint: The validation runs table may not exist yet. Run some rules first.

aiAssistant.title: Generate rules
aiAssistant.description: Describe your data quality requirements in plain language to generate rule definitions.

aiCheckGenerator.title: Generate rules
aiCheckGenerator.subtitle: Describe your data quality needs
aiCheckGenerator.enterRequirementsContext: Enter requirements for "{{name}}" to get started
aiCheckGenerator.enterRequirements: Enter your requirements below to get started
aiCheckGenerator.examplePlaceholder: Example: Sales amount must be positive
aiCheckGenerator.enterDescriptionFirst: Please enter a description of your data quality requirements
aiCheckGenerator.failedGenerate: Failed to generate checks. Please try again.

columnDiscovery.title: Column discovery
columnDiscovery.hint: Quick lookup of columns in any UC table. Click a column to copy its name; paste into the column field of any check below.
columnDiscovery.filterPlaceholder: Filter columns by name or type…
columnDiscovery.pickHint: Pick a catalog, schema, and table above to see its columns.

commentThread.deleteConfirmBody: This will permanently remove the comment. This action cannot be undone.
commentThread.dialogDescription: Discuss and leave notes for your team.

dryRun.clickRowHint: Click a row to filter the failed rows below

labelsEditor.title: Labels
labelsEditor.currentNotInCatalog: Current value {{value}} isn't in the catalog.

labelFilter.noLabelsFound: No labels found in the current view.

roleManagement.title: Entitlements
roleManagement.description: Map Databricks workspace groups to application roles. If applied to multiple entitlements, users inherit the highest level of permission.
roleManagement.addMappingHint: Add a mapping below to assign roles to Databricks groups.
roleManagement.delayTitle: Role changes take ~1 minute to take effect.
roleManagement.delayBody: Alternatively, hard refresh the page with {{shortcut}}.
roleManagement.roleAdminDescription: Full access including role management (admins are implicit runners)
roleManagement.roleApproverDescription: Can approve/reject rules and all author permissions
roleManagement.roleAuthorDescription: Can create, edit, and submit rules
roleManagement.roleRunnerDescription: Can run approved rules from the Run Rules page (additive — independent of other roles)
roleManagement.mappingAddedDescription: Changes may take ~1 minute to take effect.
roleManagement.mappingRemovedDescription: Changes may take ~1 minute to take effect.
roleManagement.fallbackError: Failed to create role mapping. Check the backend logs for details.
roleManagement.deleteMappingBody: Members of {{group}} will lose the {{role}} role. They may keep cached permissions for up to ~1 minute until their session refreshes.

rulesReview.yamlMustBeList: YAML must be a list of check definitions

runReviewPanel.label: Review status
runReviewPanel.orphanSuffix: is no longer in the catalogue. Pick a replacement.
runReviewPanel.noExplicitChanges: No explicit changes yet. The run is at the catalogue default.
runReviewPanel.orphanWarning: This run carries a value not in the current catalogue. Choose a replacement above to keep the audit trail clean.

rulesRegistry.title: Rules
rulesRegistry.description: Create a repository of reusable data quality rules.
rulesRegistry.filtersDescription: Narrow the registry by status, taxonomy, or ownership.
rulesRegistry.polarityGatedTooltip: You must apply your condition logic first
rulesRegistry.coreBasicChecksDesc: Pick a ready-made check (null, range, pattern, uniqueness, …)
rulesRegistry.coreConditionBuilderDesc: Build custom, complex conditions in low-code SQL
rulesRegistry.emptyState: No registry rules match these filters yet.
rulesRegistry.emptyStateNoRulesCta: No rules yet — click "New rule" to create one.
rulesRegistry.editAsNewDraftTooltip: This rule can't be edited directly once it leaves draft status. This creates an editable draft copy you can change and submit.
rulesRegistry.toastDraftCopyCreated: Created an editable draft copy of this rule.
rulesRegistry.jsonDialogDescriptionEditable: Saving applies these changes to a brand-new draft — this rule is left untouched.
rulesRegistry.jsonDialogDescriptionReadOnly: The native DQX check dict this rule compiles to, with reusable slots shown as placeholders instead of real columns.
rulesRegistry.jsonShapeError: Expected a check dict with a "check" object containing a "function" name.
rulesRegistry.jsonUnknownFunctionError: Unknown check function "{{function}}" — it isn't registered in DQX.
rulesRegistry.jsonDialogDescriptionApply: Applies straight back to the form — nothing is saved until you use Save / Submit below. Note: for a native check, editing the JSON re-derives canonical column slot names from the check function.
rulesRegistry.jsonAppliedToForm: Applied JSON to the form — review the fields before saving.
rulesRegistry.sqlAiWriteDescription: Describe what this predicate should check.
rulesRegistry.sqlAiWritePlaceholder: e.g. orders must have a positive amount
rulesRegistry.sqlAiExplainInserted: Added the explanation as a comment above your logic.
rulesRegistry.sqlAiWritten: AI wrote a predicate — review it before saving.
rulesRegistry.sqlAiImproved: AI refined the predicate — review it before saving.
rulesRegistry.applyModalDescription: Pick tables to monitor and apply this rule to. Slots are matched to same-named columns automatically.
rulesRegistry.applyModalToastSummary: Applied to {{applied}} table(s); {{needsMapping}} need column mapping — open each table to finish.
rulesRegistry.deleteConfirmDescription: This permanently deletes "{{name}}". This can't be undone.
rulesRegistry.rejectConfirmDescription: This sends "{{name}}" back to draft. The author can revise it and resubmit for review.
rulesRegistry.bulkApproveBody: Approved rules are published immediately and can be applied to monitored tables.
rulesRegistry.bulkDeprecateBody: Deprecated rules stay in the registry but can no longer be applied to new tables.
rulesRegistry.bulkRevokeBody: Pending submissions are withdrawn back to draft (or approved for revisions) so they can be edited and resubmitted.
rulesRegistry.bulkDeleteBody: These rules are permanently deleted. Rules currently applied to a monitored table are skipped — remove them from those tables first.
rulesRegistry.dialogSrDescription: Create or edit a reusable data quality rule.
rulesRegistry.slotsHint: Bound to real columns when this rule is applied to a monitored table.
rulesRegistry.slotsTooltip: A slot is a placeholder column (shown in curly braces). When this rule is applied to a table, you'll map each slot to a real column.
rulesRegistry.typeFamilyTooltip: Type family limits which columns can fill this slot — e.g. "numeric" only accepts number columns.
rulesRegistry.nativeHint: Pick a check function to see its slots and parameters.
rulesRegistry.sqlExplainerText: Reference columns in your query as
rulesRegistry.nativeSingleColumnTooltip: This rule's input column is set in "Columns used" above; a basic check takes a single input column.
rulesRegistry.advancedGatedTooltip: Choose a rule condition first to configure advanced options.
rulesRegistry.polarityHint: Controls whether a true predicate is a pass or a fail.
rulesRegistry.polarityTooltip: If your check is "value > 0", turn this on only if a TRUE result means the row FAILED.
rulesRegistry.polarityAriaLabel: Whether the predicate identifies passing or failing rows
rulesRegistry.missingFieldsHeader: Fill in the following before submitting:
rulesRegistry.descriptionPlaceholder: What this rule checks and why.
rulesRegistry.dimensionTooltip: Which aspect of data quality this rule measures — e.g. completeness, validity, accuracy, consistency, uniqueness, or timeliness.
rulesRegistry.severityTooltip: The default severity this rule starts with each time it's applied to a table. This can be overridden on a case-by-case basis when the rule is applied.
rulesRegistry.defaultPassThresholdHint: Optional. Checks from this rule warn when fewer than this % of rows pass, unless overridden when applied. Leave blank to use the workspace default.
rulesRegistry.unusedColumnsError: Unused declared column(s): {{columns}}. Reference them in the rule body or filter, or remove them.
rulesRegistry.canSaveMissingFieldsTooltip: Fill in the following before saving: {{fields}}.
rulesRegistry.canSubmitMissingFieldsTooltip: Fill in the following before submitting: {{fields}}.
rulesRegistry.unsavedChangesDescription: You have unsaved rule changes. Leaving will discard your edits.
rulesRegistry.aiBuildDescription: Describe the data quality requirement in plain language and review a draft before applying it.
rulesRegistry.aiBuildPlaceholder: e.g. Order amount must be positive and less than $1,000,000
rulesRegistry.aiProposalApplied: AI proposal applied — review the fields below before saving.
rulesRegistry.aiFunctionUnavailable: The AI picked a check function that isn't available here. Applied what we could — pick a function to finish the rule.
rulesRegistry.aiGenerateFailed: Failed to generate a rule proposal.
rulesRegistry.aiRateLimited: AI usage limit reached for this hour. Try again later.
rulesRegistry.aiSuggestFailed: Failed to get an AI suggestion.
rulesRegistry.aiSuggestInvalidValue: AI suggested a value that isn't one of the configured options. Pick one manually.
rulesRegistry.aiSuggestNoContextYet: No details yet — fill in a few fields first.
rulesRegistry.lowcodeAddFirstCondition: Add a condition to start building the rule.
rulesRegistry.lowcodeSqlDivergedWarning: The SQL was edited by hand, so your built conditions couldn't be recovered. Start building again from a blank condition.
rulesRegistry.lowcodeGroupByNoColumns: Declare columns first to enable grouping
rulesRegistry.lowcodeJoinPickTableDescription: Browse a catalog, schema, and table to join into this rule.
rulesRegistry.lowcodeNoCrossKeys: No join keys for a CROSS JOIN.
rulesRegistry.lowcodeConditionsRequired: Add at least one complete condition.

LOWCODE_TO_SQL.title: Switch to SQL?
LOWCODE_TO_SQL.body: We'll translate the conditions you've built into SQL and drop them into the editor.

LOWCODE_TO_NATIVE.title: Switch to DQX Native?
LOWCODE_TO_NATIVE.body: The conditions you've built in Low-Code will be cleared.

SQL_TO_LOWCODE.title: Switch to Low-Code?
SQL_TO_LOWCODE.body: Any conditions you previously built in Low-Code will be restored; SQL you edited by hand can't be rebuilt into conditions.

SQL_TO_NATIVE.title: Switch to DQX Native?
SQL_TO_NATIVE.body: The SQL predicate you wrote will be cleared.

NATIVE_TO_LOWCODE.title: Switch to Low-Code?
NATIVE_TO_LOWCODE.body: The check function you selected will be cleared.

NATIVE_TO_SQL.title: Switch to SQL?
NATIVE_TO_SQL.body: The check function you selected will be cleared.

rulesRegistry.sharingStewardHelp: The Steward is the person or group responsible for this rule's quality and lifecycle.
rulesRegistry.testNotAvailableDqxNative: Rule tests aren't available for DQX Native rules yet.
rulesRegistry.testNotAvailableAdvancedLowcode: Testing isn't available for rules with joins or grouping yet.
rulesRegistry.historyNoData: History is available once this rule has been saved.
rulesRegistry.historyVersionsTooltip: Each publish freezes an immutable snapshot. The latest version serves everywhere until a newer one is approved.
rulesRegistry.historyNoVersionsYet: No published versions yet — this rule hasn't been approved.
rulesRegistry.refTableTooltip: The Unity Catalog table this rule checks against (e.g. the parent table for a foreign key).
rulesRegistry.typeChangeWarnBody: Conditions using {{columns}} rely on the current data type. Changing it will clear those conditions. Continue?
rulesRegistry.refColumnsTooltip: Columns on the reference table used by this check.
rulesRegistry.slotsPanelTooltip: A placeholder for a column you'll choose when the rule is applied to a table.
rulesRegistry.singleColumnFnAddExplainer: Only the top column applies to this rule; the rest are only used in advanced filter conditions.
rulesRegistry.slotsPanelEmpty: Add generic columns to refer to in your rule (e.g. {{amount}}).
rulesRegistry.slotFamilyLockedTooltip: This check only works on one column type, so its family is fixed.
rulesRegistry.resultsNotAppliedTooltip: This rule must be applied to at least one monitored table before results are available.
rulesRegistry.resultsScoreErrorTooltip: Couldn't check whether this rule is applied anywhere — click to retry.
rulesRegistry.resultsNotApplied: This rule isn't applied to any monitored table yet, so there are no results to show.
rulesRegistry.resultsNoAccessibleTables_one: This rule is applied to {{count}} monitored table you don't have access to.
rulesRegistry.resultsNoAccessibleTables_other: This rule is applied to {{count}} monitored tables you don't have access to.

monitoredTables.title: Tables
monitoredTables.subtitle: Apply rules to specific tables and monitor their data quality.
monitoredTables.emptyState: No tables match these filters yet.
monitoredTables.emptyStateNoTablesCta: Not monitoring any tables yet — click "Monitor a table" to get started.
monitoredTables.deleteConfirmDescription: This removes "{{table}}" from Rules Registry governance and deletes every applied rule and materialized check tied to it. This can't be undone.
monitoredTables.stewardOptionalHint: Optional — the person responsible for this table's data quality.
monitoredTables.aboutColumnJumpTooltip: Go to Apply Rules for this column
monitoredTables.aboutLoadFailedHint: The table may have been dropped or you may not have access to it in Unity Catalog.
monitoredTables.submitDisabledNoChangesHint: No changes to submit — this table's checks are already approved.
monitoredTables.submitDisabledNeedsDraftRunHint: A draft run is required before you submit for review
monitoredTables.submitDisabledStaleDraftRunHint: You cannot submit for approval without first running your draft
monitoredTables.toastSubmitFailed: Failed to submit table for review.
monitoredTables.pendingBannerBody: This table's checks have been submitted and are awaiting an approver's review. They will not run until approved.
monitoredTables.rejectConfirmDescription: This rejects every pending check submitted for "{{table}}". The steward can revise and resubmit for review.
monitoredTables.unsavedChangesDescription: You have unsaved changes to this table's applied rules. Leaving will discard your edits.
monitoredTables.noProfileHint: Run the profiler on this table from Profile & Generate to see column statistics here.
monitoredTables.profileEmptyHint: Run the profiler to see column statistics for this table.
monitoredTables.columnLensUnavailable: Column lens needs Unity Catalog access — switch back to By rule.
monitoredTables.addRuleForColumnHint: Pick a rule to apply to column "{{column}}".
monitoredTables.emptyAppliedRules: No rules applied to this table yet.

pendingApplications.description: These registry rules were staged against this table by bulk import but aren't applied yet. They auto-apply once the rule is approved.

monitoredTables.checksViaRulesTooltip: A rule is a piece of reusable logic. A check is when a rule gets applied to a column/set of columns. A test is when a check runs against a data row.
monitoredTables.ruleLogicUnavailable: No logic details available for this rule.
monitoredTables.pinStaleTooltip: Newer version available (v{{latest}}). This rule is pinned to v{{pinned}} and runs that exact frozen version.
monitoredTables.removeConfirmDescription: This deletes "{{name}}" from this table, including any materialized checks. This can't be undone.
monitoredTables.addRuleDialogDescription: Pick one or more published registry rules to apply to this table.
monitoredTables.noPublishedRules: No published rules match your search.
monitoredTables.createRuleInlineHint: Select an existing rule or <link>create a new one</link>.
monitoredTables.noMatchingColumns: No columns of this type on this table.
monitoredTables.applyToTagTooltip: Apply tags to this column to auto-apply this rule to any column of a monitored table with matching tag(s)
monitoredTables.resultsDescription: Latest data quality score and a sample of failing records from this table's runs. Full run history lives in Runs History.
monitoredTables.notYetPublishedResultsHint: Submit this table for review to materialize its applied rules into checks before results appear here.
monitoredTables.resultsApprovalBannerBody_one: {{count}} check materialized from this table's applied rules is not yet approved and will not run until an approver reviews it.
monitoredTables.resultsApprovalBannerBody_other: {{count}} checks materialized from this table's applied rules are not yet approved and will not run until an approver reviews them.
monitoredTables.invalidFqnHint: Select a table from the catalog browser.
monitoredTables.suggestRulesDialogDescription: Published rules that match this table's columns. Review each mapping before you add it.
monitoredTables.suggestRulesUnavailableTitle: AI suggestions aren't available right now.
monitoredTables.suggestRulesUnavailableFallback: You can still add and map rules manually.
monitoredTables.suggestRulesEmptyTitle: No strong matches found for this table.
monitoredTables.suggestRulesEmptyDescription: The suggestion engine only returns rules it's confident about. Add rules manually when you know what you need.
monitoredTables.suggestRulesAddFailed: Failed to add some suggested rules.
monitoredTables.suggestRulesTimeoutReason: The suggestion request timed out. Check your connection and try again.
monitoredTables.suggestRulesTableUnavailableReason: The table's columns couldn't be read from Unity Catalog, so suggestions can't be matched yet. Suggestions will resume once the catalog/schema/table is reachable.
monitoredTables.suggestRulesNoneSelected: Select at least one suggestion to add.

wizard.title: Monitor tables
wizard.description: Select table(s) to apply rules to and monitor
wizard.scopeWarningManyTables_one: {{count}} table will be added. This may take a while.
wizard.scopeWarningManyTables_other: {{count}} tables will be added. This may take a while.
wizard.confirmDescription_one: This will apply the rule to {{count}} table. Continue?
wizard.confirmDescription_other: This will apply the rule to {{count}} tables. Continue?
wizard.toastAttachRulesHint: Open each monitored table to attach rules.
wizard.lockedTabHint: Available after you save this monitor.

monitoredTables.emptyTable: No tables are being monitored yet. Click "Monitor a table" to get started.
monitoredTables.runNowDisabledHint: Approve this table's checks first, then run its approved version.
monitoredTables.runInProgressHint: A run is already in progress. Wait for it to finish before starting another.
monitoredTables.runDraftDisabledHint: Run draft is available in draft, or when you have unsaved edits to save and run.
monitoredTables.historyEmpty: No history yet — a version is frozen each time this table is approved.
monitoredTables.scheduleFooterNote: Schedule changes take effect as soon as you save
monitoredTables.scheduleEmptyText: This table runs on demand only. Add a schedule to run it automatically.
monitoredTables.thresholdPillMixedAria: Warning threshold: mixed — per-column overrides differ from the rule default
monitoredTables.thresholdPillAria: Warning threshold: warn when fewer than {{pct}}% of tests pass
monitoredTables.thresholdPopoverHint: Warn when fewer than this % of tests pass this check.
monitoredTables.columnThresholdPopoverHint: Warn when fewer than this % of tests pass this rule for this column.
monitoredTables.scheduleCrossDescription: This table belongs to the space(s) below, whose schedules run its checks too. Any schedule you set here runs in addition to those.

viewData.loadFailed: Couldn't load table data. Check the SQL warehouse and your access.
viewData.queryFailed: Couldn't run that query. Try rephrasing your question.
viewData.askPlaceholder: Ask a question about this data…

monitoredTables.bulkRunBody: Run the approved version of the selected tables now.
monitoredTables.bulkApproveBody: Approved tables are published immediately and their checks become runnable.
monitoredTables.bulkRejectBody: Pending submissions are rejected. The steward can revise and resubmit.
monitoredTables.bulkDeleteBody: This removes the selected tables from monitoring and deletes every applied rule and materialized check tied to them. This can't be undone.

dataProducts.title: Spaces
dataProducts.subtitle: Group related tables into spaces with a shared schedule.
dataProducts.emptyState: No spaces match these filters yet.
dataProducts.emptyStateNoProductsCta: No spaces yet — click "New Space" to get started.
dataProducts.scheduleCronHelp: Five fields: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week. Example: 0 6 * * * runs daily at 06:00.
dataProducts.scheduleCronInvalid: This cron expression isn't valid. Check the format and try again.
dataProducts.scheduleEmptyText: This space runs on demand only. Add a schedule to run it automatically.
dataProducts.scheduleFooterNote: Schedule changes take effect as soon as you save
dataProducts.detailPlaceholder: Full product details — tables, sharing, schedule, and runs — are coming soon.
dataProducts.stewardHelp: The steward owns data quality for this product and reviews rule changes.
dataProducts.reviewChangesDescription: These member tables have submitted checks awaiting approval. Approve or reject each directly, or open the table for full context.
dataProducts.runNowDisabledHint: Add a table that's approved and versioned first.
dataProducts.runInProgressHint: A run is already in progress. Wait for it to finish before starting another.
dataProducts.runDraftDisabledHint: Run draft is available in draft, or when you have unsaved edits to save and run.
dataProducts.deleteConfirmDescription: This deletes the space and stops its scheduled runs. Your tables and their own monitoring aren't affected. This cannot be undone.
dataProducts.unsavedChangesDescription: Leaving now discards your unsaved edits to this space.
dataProducts.tabPlaceholder: This section is coming in a later update.
dataProducts.toastRunNoneStarted: Run couldn't start — no members could be launched.
dataProducts.tablesEmptyState: No tables yet. Add tables to include them in this product.
dataProducts.memberNotReadyHelper: Not fully set up to run checks yet — open it to finish setup.
dataProducts.removeTableConfirmDescription: Remove {{table}} from this product? Its own monitoring isn't affected.
dataProducts.addTablesDescription: Select monitored tables to add to this space.
dataProducts.pickerNoTables: No monitored tables available yet. Go to Tables to start monitoring one, then add it here.
dataProducts.pickerNoMatches: No tables match the current filters.
dataProducts.pickerNotEligibleTooltip: Only approved tables can join a space. Submit this table for review and approve it first.
dataProducts.pinStaleTooltip: Newer version available (v{{latest}}). This member is pinned to v{{pinned}} and runs that exact frozen version.
dataProducts.runsEmpty: No runs yet. Click "Run now" to start one.
dataProducts.runsEmptyNotRunnable: Add a table that's approved and versioned, then run this product to see results here.
dataProducts.pendingBannerBody: This space's changes have been submitted and are awaiting an approver's review. They will not take effect until approved.
dataProducts.submitDisabledNoChangesHint: Nothing to submit — already approved with no unsaved changes.
dataProducts.submitDisabledNeedsDraftRunHint: A draft run is required before you submit for review
dataProducts.submitDisabledStaleDraftRunHint: You cannot submit for approval without first running your draft
dataProducts.rejectConfirmDescription: Reject "{{name}}"? The author can revise it and submit it for review again.
dataProducts.bulkRunBody: Run the approved version of the selected spaces now.
dataProducts.bulkApproveBody: Approved spaces are published immediately and can be run.
dataProducts.bulkRejectBody: Pending submissions are rejected. The author can revise and resubmit.
dataProducts.bulkDeleteBody: This deletes the selected spaces and stops their scheduled runs. Your tables and their own monitoring aren't affected. This cannot be undone.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.stewardHelp: The steward owns data quality for this object and reviews changes.
permissions.defaultGrantHint: The workspace users group can view and apply this object by default. Narrow or revoke this to restrict access.
permissions.ownerGrantHint: The creator owns this object and has full access by default. This is granted automatically and can't be changed here.
permissions.inheritToggleHint: When on, this grant flows down to member tables.
permissions.inheritToggleHintMonitoredTable: When on, this grant also flows down to the table's underlying rules.
permissions.inheritToggleHintTableSpace: When on, this grant also flows down to member tables and their rules.
permissions.emptyStateRule: Save the rule to apply permissions.
permissions.emptyStateTable: Save the table to apply permissions.
permissions.emptyStateTableSpace: Save the space to apply permissions.

ruleTest.noRows: No rows returned for this sample.
ruleTest.truncatedNote: Showing the first {{count}} rows; evaluation ran over the full sample.
ruleTest.addDefinitionToTest: Add a rule definition to test.

globalResults.title: Results
globalResults.subtitle: Organization-wide data quality across every monitored table you can access.

results.suppressedFineGrainedControls: Failing records are hidden because this table uses fine-grained access controls (a row filter or column mask), which the sample cannot faithfully reproduce.

resultsUi.scoreTrendUpAria: Score improved vs the previous run
resultsUi.scoreTrendDownAria: Score fell vs the previous run
resultsUi.runInProgressBanner: A run is in progress. Results will refresh automatically when it finishes.
resultsUi.noFailureDetails: No failure details were recorded for this row.
resultsUi.failedRecordsCaptionCapped: Showing {{range}} of first {{loaded}} failed records. Download to view all {{total}} total failed records.
resultsUi.countInfo: A rule is a piece of reusable logic. A check is when a rule gets applied to a column/set of columns. A test is when a check runs against a data row
resultsUi.appliedCountsTitle: Number of Applied Rules, Checks & Tests
resultsUi.failedCountsTitle: Number of Failed Rules, Checks, Tests & Rows
resultsUi.pickTableForColumns: Pick a table to view columns
resultsUi.invalidSamplesUnavailable: Invalid samples can't be shown for this table.

genie.title: Ask Genie
genie.openSpaceTitle: Open the full Genie space in a new tab
